headline: Europeans rethink collective defense amid Trump's threats against NATO
description: Would the US intervene in the event of an attack on Europe? European leaders, who met Monday in Yerevan alongside Canada, are growing increasingly doubtful after Trump announced the withdrawal of 5,000 American troops from Germany.

headline: US and Iran escalate 'double blockade' of Strait of Hormuz
Hundreds of flight cancellations leave passengers frustrated and stranded
Sarkozy's counter-offensive leaves judges unconvinced at Libyan funding trial
description: The former French president once again tried on Monday to prove that the sums paid by Libyans were not used to fund his 2007 presidential campaign.

headline: European Commission shelves plan to tighten regulation of chemical products
description: Promised six years ago as part of the European Union's Green Deal, the revision of the regulation on hazardous chemicals will ultimately not see the light of day. Since the project was announced, industry lobby groups have worked to limit its scope.

headline: How a dream cruise turned into a health nightmare
description: Hantavirus is responsible for the death of at least one of the three passengers who died this past month. While another three people on board are showing symptoms of acute respiratory distress, the luxury cruise ship, which Cape Verdean authorities have banned from docking, could head for the Canary Islands.

headline: Military personnel at French presidency suspected of compromising classified documents
description: Prosecutors are investigating several members of the military and civilians suspected of setting up a private company to sell classified information and weapons of war. Six suspects have been charged.

Saint Pierre and Miquelon, a pioneering example of climate change adaptation
description: This year, residents of a town in the French overseas territory off the coast of Canada are set to move into a new village. This relocation, France's first planned effort to move an entire town, is the result of a lengthy process.

headline: The Eames House, an enduring utopia spared by the Los Angeles fires
description: In 1949, design icons Ray and Charles Eames built a villa-workshop in Los Angeles that laid the foundation for a new kind of architecture. The landmark building miraculously survived the massive January 2025 fire and was recently celebrated at Milan's Design Week.

headline: The 'alpha male' holds little appeal for women (and comes at a high cost)
description: While many young men are receptive to the hyper-masculine rhetoric that is spread across social media, the stereotypes touted as ways to attract women rarely succeed, writes columnist Maïa Mazaurette. Instead, she encourages men to take a genuine interest in women.
